title: Apple's Enterprise IT Battle Plan, Part Two
author: Elizabeth Millard (reporter, MacNewsWorld); Peter Kastner (quoted source)
abstract: MacNewsWorld second installment on Apple's enterprise strategy. Peter Kastner of Aberdeen Group sees Apple 'turning up the heat' by targeting specialized verticals (wireless, online business) rather than head-on assaults on Fortune 500 IT shops. Kastner argues that large-enterprise IS staffs gravitate away from Apple because they are trained to wrangle complex multi-vendor stacks, making small and medium-sized businesses the more natural beachhead for Apple enterprise penetration.
importance_rationale: Companion piece to Part One; captures Kastner's SMB-first enterprise strategy thesis for Apple at the Tiger-launch era, which framed subsequent trade-press coverage.
relevance_rationale: Apple's eventual enterprise entry came via mobile (iPhone/iPad BYOD) and SMB rather than via Xserve/OS X Server — consistent with Kastner's framing. Still useful as a primary record of the strategy debate.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's SMB-first prediction and 'specialized verticals' thesis tracked with reality: Apple exited Xserve (2011) and large-enterprise server plays, but iPhone/iPad drove SMB and vertical (healthcare, retail, logistics) adoption that ultimately delivered enterprise relevance.
